programme.desc: A note indicates that class has been moved to the science lab. The lab is unoccupied, but Jan Di waits, not realizing that the room is filling with fumes.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's father gets kidnapped by loan sharks, which inevitably forces Jan Di's mother to swallow her pride and plead for Madam Kang's help.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: The F4 guys have moved on to college while Jan Di has entered her senior year of high school. She has not heard from Jun Pyo since he left.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di waits to hear the conclusions of F3's meeting with Jun-pyo, and can tell from their look that it was not good.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: After treating Jan Di in a cruel manner, Mr. Jung prepares everything so that Jun-Pyo can visit her in Seoul. Will he be able to go without Madam Kang finding out?

programme.title: The World of Us
programme.desc: A bullied girl becomes friends with a new kid during a break, but when classes start up again, their new friendship is nearly ruined when the bullying continues.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's family hears about Jun-pyo's engagement and asks her miserably if it's true.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Ji-hoo can see how hurt Jan Di is by the kiss, and continues to follow her as she stumbles around in shock.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo begins seeing Jae-Kyung in a new light, like he's seeing her for the first time as a potential friend -- or maybe a kindred spirit.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Woo-bin tries to talk some sense into Yi-jung, who is determined to drink himself into a stupor.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: At home, Jun-hee reminisces about how her mother meddled in her life, in much the same way she's interfering in Jun-pyo's now.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi-jung is waiting outside for Eun-jae, excited to see her -- until he listens in on her phone conversation; from her words, it sounds like a call to a boyfriend.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo begs for Jan Di to advise him not to get married.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo tells Jan Di that he'll protect her, but to Jun-pyo's surprise, she says that she does not want to be cosseted or taken care of.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Perhaps feeling it's time to expose one truth, Mr. Jung leads Jun-hee to the unknown patient's bedside; at the sight of her father, Jun-hee breaks down.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo asks Yumi to make the lunchbox once more, confirming that Yumi had taken credit for her lunch.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Just being rich isn't enough to endure the teen oppression at Shinhwa -- loners are often marked as targets by the bored über-clique called F4.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A wicked rumor is spread on the Internet that a second-year student at Shinhwa High School is pregnant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Not too eager to watch Jan Di dancing with one of his best friends, Jun Pyo exits the ballroom and ends up taking out his frustrations by the poolside.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Making a rash choice, Jan Di tells her mother she has to go somewhere and runs off, heading toward Namsan Tower.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's bike is set ablaze while she is attacked with water balloons and blasted with a fire extinguisher.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A psychic foresees that Jan Di will be losing something significant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun Pyo intends to expel his best friend and Jan Di out of Shinhwa High School -- and out of F4.

programme.desc: Yi-jung gets a frantic call from his mother threatening to commit suicide because of his father.

programme.desc: Woo-bin tries to talk some sense into Yi-jung, who is determined to drink himself into a stupor.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: At home, Jun-hee reminisces about how her mother meddled in her life, in much the same way she's interfering in Jun-pyo's now.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi-jung is waiting outside for Eun-jae, excited to see her -- until he listens in on her phone conversation; from her words, it sounds like a call to a boyfriend.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo begs for Jan Di to advise him not to get married.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo tells Jan Di that he'll protect her, but to Jun-pyo's surprise, she says that she does not want to be cosseted or taken care of.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Perhaps feeling it's time to expose one truth, Mr. Jung leads Jun-hee to the unknown patient's bedside; at the sight of her father, Jun-hee breaks down.

programme.title: Harada: Sculpting for Peace
programme.desc: France, summer 2019. The Japanese artist Tetsuo Harada has just turned 70 years old. He has spent his life carving works for peace and respect for nature in his 3000 m² workshop. He works tirelessly on new projects.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo asks Yumi to make the lunchbox once more, confirming that Yumi had taken credit for her lunch.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Just being rich isn't enough to endure the teen oppression at Shinhwa -- loners are often marked as targets by the bored über-clique called F4.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A wicked rumor is spread on the Internet that a second-year student at Shinhwa High School is pregnant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Not too eager to watch Jan Di dancing with one of his best friends, Jun Pyo exits the ballroom and ends up taking out his frustrations by the poolside.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Making a rash choice, Jan Di tells her mother she has to go somewhere and runs off, heading toward Namsan Tower.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's bike is set ablaze while she is attacked with water balloons and blasted with a fire extinguisher.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A psychic foresees that Jan Di will be losing something significant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun Pyo intends to expel his best friend and Jan Di out of Shinhwa High School -- and out of F4.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di dashes to the hospital after being informed that Jun Pyo had been in an accident.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi-jung gets a frantic call from his mother threatening to commit suicide because of his father.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi Jung's mother frantically calls him threatening to commit suicide.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A note indicates that class has been moved to the science lab. The lab is unoccupied, but Jan Di waits, not realizing that the room is filling with fumes.

programme.desc: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly talk to NBA Hall of Famer Tracy McGrady to discuss how he became an NFL owner, why the '90s revolutionized basketball and returning to the NBA as an NBC analyst.

programme.title: Insight With Haslinda Amin
programme.desc: The definitive guide for global news audiences to get the complete picture on the story of the day, through probing interviews and nuanced conversations.

programme.title: Bloomberg Horizons Middle East & Africa
programme.desc: A spotlight on the fast-paced growth and major deals happening across the Middle East and Africa, linking the market drivers from overnight in the U.S. and across Asia into the region's trading day, with deep-dive analysis of the top businesses.

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe
programme.desc: From London, essential morning viewing to stay ahead of the day's market trends with news stories from the U.S. and Asia in the lead-up to the opening of European markets.

programme.title: Bloomberg The Opening Trade
programme.desc: "The Opening Trade" explores things to know as markets open across Europe; analysis that isn't available anywhere else, a break-down of the biggest stories of the day and top guests who have skin in the game.

programme.title: The Pulse with Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Based in London, Francine Lacqua speaks with guests about global business, economics, finance and politics.

programme.title: Bloomberg Brief
programme.desc: "Bloomberg Brief" delivers the market news, data and analysis viewers need to set their agendas.

programme.title: Bloomberg Surveillance
programme.desc: Jonathan, Lisa and Annmarie survey the economy and global markets with daily conversations with leaders and decision makers, from Wall Street to Washington and beyond, to cover the latest in business, investment and geopolitics.

programme.title: Bloomberg Open Interest
programme.desc: Delivering the market-moving news anyone needs to know to get a jump start on the U.S. trading day.

programme.title: Bloomberg Money
programme.desc: Breaking news and projections before the start of the business day.

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: Focusing on the intersection of politics and global business.

programme.title: Bloomberg Businessweek Daily
programme.desc: Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec bring reporting from the magazine that helps global leaders stay ahead, plus insight on the people, companies and trends shaping today's complex economy.

programme.title: Bloomberg: The Close
programme.desc: Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld break down the day's top market-moving stories and trading action around the closing bell.

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: Focusing on the intersection of politics and global business.

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week
programme.desc: A weekly program telling the stories of capitalism from around the world, David Westin in New York hosts.

programme.title: Latitude
programme.desc: Haslinda Amin talks to the people helping to shape Asia's rise, travelling the region to meet the businesspeople, top talent, entrepreneurs and leaders that are forging its future.

programme.title: The Mishal Husain Show
programme.desc: Mishal Husain brings her signature blend of curiosity and tenacity to weekly conversations with world leaders, business titans and cultural icons, revealing who they really are and how they see the world changing around them.

programme.title: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: The Podcast
programme.sub-title: Rolls-Royce CEO Tufan Erginbilgiç on Transforming a British Icon
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua speaks with Rolls-Royce Chief Executive Officer Tufan Erginbilgiç about confronting the company's weaknesses with data-driven honesty and setting a clear, granular strategy to transform performance and culture.

programme.title: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: The Podcast
programme.sub-title: Blackstone's Jon Gray on Talent, Loyalty and Building a $1.3 Trillion Wall Street Giant
programme.desc: Jon Gray emphasizes the importance of resilience, clear decision-making and maintaining humanity in leadership, especially during tough times.

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations
programme.desc: Al Gore, Former US Vice President, Climate Activist.

programme.title: Bloomberg Originals
programme.desc: Experts offer bold takes on some of today's most important topics and cover stories and investigations at the intersection of business and culture.

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week
programme.desc: A weekly program telling the stories of capitalism from around the world, David Westin in New York hosts.

programme.title: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
programme.sub-title: FIFA's Jill Ellis on World Cup Demand
programme.desc: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly host conversations with business moguls, entrepreneurs and sports champions who reveal the secrets behind their success.

programme.title: USA 250 Gold Rarity
programme.desc: Celebrate the 250th Anniversary of American Freedom with this Gold 1776-2026 American Gold Eagle graded PF70UCAM. Encapsulated in a 1 of 250 40th Anniversary holder with the First Day of Issue designation, this is a once in a lifetime American coin!

programme.desc: A businessman embarks on a revenge-fueled vendetta after his daughter dies in a terrorist bombing. His search leads to a cat-and-mouse conflict with a British government official whose past may hold the clues to the identities of the elusive killers.

programme.title: A Force of One
programme.desc: A veteran (Chuck Norris) teaches martial arts to a California detective (Jennifer O'Neill) and her colleagues, and joins them on a case.

programme.title: The Asian Connection
programme.desc: Two American expats, Jack and Sam, become the targets of a Southeast Asian gang's vengeance when they unwittingly steal a drug lord's money.

programme.title: Bangkok Dangerous
programme.desc: A hitman who's in Bangkok to pull off a series of jobs violates his personal code when he falls for a local woman and bonds with his errand boy.

programme.title: Primal
programme.desc: Hunter and collector Frank Walsh sails to America with a priceless white jaguar and a dangerous political assassin. When the prisoner manages to break free, Walsh must utilize his expert skills to capture the dangerous killer -- not dead, but alive.

Officer Mike Chandler and a young civilian passenger find themselves unprepared and outgunned when fate puts them squarely in-the-crosshairs of a daring bank heist that is being carried out by a fearless team of highly trained and heavily armed men.

programme.title: Drive Angry 3D
programme.desc: A vengeful father escapes from hell and chases after the men who killed his daughter and kidnapped his granddaughter.

programme.title: The Vatican Tapes
programme.desc: The fate of the world hangs in the balance when a cleric (Peter Andersson) battles an ancient satanic force for a young woman's (Olivia Taylor Dudley) soul.

programme.title: Children of the Corn: Genesis
programme.desc: Seeking refuge at a secluded compound, a couple (Kelen Coleman, Tim Rock) encounters a mysterious cult that worships a possessed boy.

programme.title: Children of the Corn: Runaway
programme.desc: A pregnant woman escapes a murderous child cult in a small town and spends the next decade living anonymously to protect her son. After they move to a new town, she realizes something evil is following them, and she must confront it to save them.

programme.title: From Dusk Till Dawn
programme.desc: As Kate nears death, the team races to defeat Amaru and seal the gate to Xibalba.

programme.title: From Dusk Till Dawn
programme.desc: Bank robbers Seth and Richie Gecko head for the Mexican border, encountering a deadly standoff at a liquor store along the way; Richie is haunted by visions of demons and a mysterious woman.

programme.title: From Dusk Till Dawn
programme.desc: The Gecko brothers flee the liquor store with Ranger Freddie Gonzalez in pursuit; Richie's visions escalate; the Geckos nearly collide with the Fullers, a troubled family making its own desperate journey to Mexico.

programme.title: Bangkok Dog
programme.desc: Special agent Andrew Kang intercepts a drug shipment and goes undercover in Bangkok's criminal underworld. Posing as a cartel operative, he must gain the gang's trust, but as the lines blur, he risks losing himself-and his way out.

programme.title: Street Smart
programme.desc: A New York journalist (Christopher Reeve) lies when his fake story about a pimp describes a real pimp (Morgan Freeman) up for murder.

programme.title: On the Streets of L.A.
programme.desc: An estranged father and son are paroled from the same maximum-security prison with one unusual condition -- they must become roommates.

programme.title: Miss Sharon Jones!
programme.desc: Filmmaker Barbara Kopple follows rhythm and blues singer Sharon Jones as she tries to hold her band together while battling pancreatic cancer.

programme.title: Hurricane Season
programme.desc: One year after Hurricane Katrina, coach Al Collins (Forest Whitaker) gathers displaced students and turns them into a championship basketball team.

programme.title: Akeelah and the Bee
programme.desc: Akeelah, an 11-year-old girl living in South Los Angeles, discovers she has a talent for spelling, which she hopes will take her to the National Spelling Bee.

programme.title: Beyond the Lights
programme.desc: A troubled star and an aspiring politician fall deeply in love, but those around the couple urge them to put their careers ahead of romance.
A Los Angeles high-school teacher (Samuel L. Jackson) confronts violent students with a grudge against him and society.

programme.title: Youngblood
programme.desc: An angry Vietnam veteran (Lawrence-Hilton Jacobs) leads a Los Angeles street gang in a war on drug dealers.

programme.title: Triple Threat
programme.desc: A crime syndicate places a hit on a billionaire's daughter, making her the target of an elite assassin squad. A band of down-and-out mercenaries protects her, fighting tooth and nail to stop the assassins from reaching their target.

programme.title: As Good as Dead
programme.desc: Bryant befriends a troubled teen and introduces him to martial arts. As Bryant's mysterious and dangerous past catches up to him, he is forced into a life and death struggle to clear his name, save the boy and get back all he left behind.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: A former Marine (Michael Jai White) travels to Brazil to hunt down the brutal Japanese mobsters who attacked his sister and left her for dead.

programme.title: Chaos
programme.desc: A veteran detective and a rookie cop have to stop a master thief's computer virus before it drains all the funds from a bank's accounts.

programme.title: Armed Response
programme.desc: Trained operatives find themselves trapped inside an isolated military compound after its artificial intelligence is suddenly shut down. There, they begin to experience strange and horrific phenomena.

programme.title: Empire State
programme.desc: A security guard (Liam Hemsworth) and his childhood pal (Michael Angarano) make plans to rob an armored car.

programme.title: A Quiet Storm
programme.desc: A gifted teenager from New Orleans is torn between family loyalty and doing what she knows is right after discovering her older cousin is a violent criminal.

programme.title: The Ride
programme.desc: Released from juvenile detention, a troubled teen enters the world of extreme BMX bike riding with help from his foster father.

programme.title: Beat Street
programme.desc: A student composer (Rae Dawn Chong) inspires South Bronx residents to new heights in break dancing, rap music and graffiti.

programme.title: As Good as Dead
programme.desc: Bryant befriends a troubled teen and introduces him to martial arts. As Bryant's mysterious and dangerous past catches up to him, he is forced into a life and death struggle to clear his name, save the boy and get back all he left behind.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: A former Marine (Michael Jai White) travels to Brazil to hunt down the brutal Japanese mobsters who attacked his sister and left her for dead.

programme.title: Blue Hill Avenue
programme.desc: Four Boston friends (Allen Payne, Aaron D. Spears) become big-time drug dealers but face the wrath of another kingpin.

programme.title: Cut Throat City
programme.desc: When Hurricane Katrina devastates New Orleans, four desperate friends band together to pull off a daring heist in the heart of the city.

programme.title: State Property
programme.desc: A Philadelphia gang member (Beanie Sigel) becomes a drug kingpin and starts a feud with vicious rivals.

programme.title: Caught in the Crossfire
programme.desc: Two detectives (Chris Klein, Richard T. Jones) are targeted by gangsters and crooked cops as they investigate an important case.

programme.title: The Island
programme.desc: When his brother is killed, LAPD officer Mark leaves the city to return to the island he grew up on. Seeking answers and vengeance, he soon finds himself in a bloody battle with the corrupt tycoon who's taken over the island paradise.

programme.title: Home of the Brave
programme.desc: Three soldiers, including a doctor (Samuel L. Jackson), have difficulty adjusting to life at home following a long and difficult tour of duty in Iraq.

programme.title: The Life and Death of Tupac Shakur
programme.desc: Seen as a poet whose tales of urban alienation captivated young people of all races and backgrounds, Tupac Shakur sets the stage for the current and continuing evolution of the hip-hop phenomenon.

programme.title: Phantom Punch
programme.desc: Ex-convict Sonny Liston (Ving Rhames) becomes the world heavyweight boxing champion in 1962, but his personal life is marred by run-ins with organized crime and the law.

programme.title: Final Shot: The Hank Gathers Story
programme.desc: Inspired by his mother (Nell Carter) and a priest (George Kennedy) in Philadelphia, he (Victor Love) rises to basketball All-American at Loyola Marymount.

programme.title: Crossover
programme.desc: The lives of a gifted athlete (Wesley Jonathan) and his best friend (Anthony Mackie) change when they take a fateful trip to Los Angeles.

programme.title: Sprung
programme.desc: Friends of each partner conspire to break up new lovers (Tisha Campbell, Rusty Cundieff), then to reconcile them.

programme.title: 4Play
programme.desc: Unsatisfied with his prior romantic conquests, a Chicago club owner (Wesley Jonathan) tries to juggle relationships with three women at once.

programme.title: House Arrest
programme.desc: DeAndre (Jayceon Taylor) becomes involved in a computer chip scheme, and his girlfriend is charged with the crime and placed under house arrest.

programme.title: School Dance
programme.desc: A teen (Bobb'e J. Thompson) hopes to impress a girl he likes by winning a spot on his school's hottest dance crew.

programme.title: Held Up
programme.desc: Yuppie Alex (Jamie Foxx) stops for gas at a remote convenience store, gets dumped by his fiancée (Nia Long) and becomes a hostage during a robbery.

programme.title: Stan Helsing
programme.desc: Stan (Steve Howey), a slacker video store clerk, his sexy girlfriend, best friend and an exotic dancer take a detour into a town cursed by the biggest monsters in movie history.

programme.title: Leprechaun in the Hood
programme.desc: When three rappers want to get even with a pimp, they accidentally unleash a leprechaun who goes on a killing rampage in tha 'hood.

programme.title: Leprechaun: Back 2 tha Hood
programme.desc: When Emily Woodrow and her friends happen on a treasure chest full of gold coins, they encounter trouble with a very nasty and protective Leprechaun.

programme.desc: La boda civil entre María de los Ángeles y Rodrigo está a punto de comenzar.

programme.title: María de los Ángeles
programme.desc: Una joven debe enfrentar a su tía para reclamar sus derechos y fortuna y vive situaciones difíciles.

programme.title: Mi gorda bella
programme.desc: Lorenzo hace que Román salga de la cárcel y así puedan liberar a su esposa secuestrada. Luego de un año en recuperación, Valentina cambia su estado físico.

programme.title: Mi gorda bella
programme.desc: Valentina siente un tremendo odio y tristeza después de que Orestes intentó envenenarla. Además, decide olvidar todo y nunca más regresar a Venezuela.

programme.title: Juana, la virgen
programme.desc: Juana se muestra menos distante ante la fragilidad del padre de su hijo. Mauricio insiste en validar su verdadero amor hacia ella.

programme.title: Juana, la virgen
programme.desc: Mientras Eva conduce, Juana se ha quedado dormida. Al sentir que el carro se detiene, la joven se despierta y descubre que están frente a la cabaña de la montaña.

programme.title: Piel salvaje
programme.desc: Roberto intenta abusar de Camila en la bodega de la empresa, llega el chofer de Ezequiel y golpea a Roberto por violentar a Camila. El hijo de Ezequiel duda de que su padre sea un asesino, pero las pruebas están enfrente de él.

programme.title: Piel salvaje
programme.desc: Ezequiel apunta con pistola a Roberto para que confiese que viene de una banda y por eso trató de abusar de Camila. Camila le cuenta la verdad sobre Gabriel y su asesinato a Max. Julia y Celso anuncian a su familia que se van a casar.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's 5 Ingredient Mediterranean Meals
programme.desc: Jamie hacks his oven into a rotisserie for crispy chicken and chips and bakes a savory spinach and feta pie. Then, the Godfather of Spanish cuisine, Jose Pizarro, shares some gorgeous Mediterranean flavours using just 5 everyday ingredients.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Seasons
programme.sub-title: Buddy's Strawberry Rice Pudding and Summer Tomato Chicken Bake
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a chicken sheet pan dinner with early tomatoes, potatoes and chorizo. Buddy helps pick strawberries for quick jam rice pudding. The summer feast ends with spicy shrimp skewers with homegrown chillies and a refreshing cucumber gazpacho.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Seasons
programme.sub-title: Endless Summer: From Grilled Panzanella Salad to Sour Cherry Tart
programme.desc: Jamie ends summer with crispy duck in a rich plum sauce & roast potatoes. Grilled peppers star in panzanella. Garden zucchini become Mediterranean rice. Sweet finale: sour cherry frangipane with crème fraîche.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Hearty Chicken Pot Pie and French Toast Crumpets with Bacon
programme.desc: From a cosy chicken hotpot with braised cabbage to cheesy French-toast crumpets with smashed peas and bacon, a quick, aromatic fish curry, and a golden pear & almond cake with orange drizzle, Jamie proves simple ingredients really can shine.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Caprese Chicken Burgers and Pork and Fennel Ragu
programme.desc: From crispy, herby Caprese chicken burgers to golden spinach pies, creamy pork & fennel ragù with fresh pappardelle, and a comforting tinned peach cobbler with fruit-rippled cream, Jamie proves budget-friendly cooking can be utterly delicious.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a hearty chicken and veg pie, super-satisfying mushroom risotto, beautiful homemade bread and a no-cook chocolatey fridge cake, all for under a buck a plate.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Seasons
programme.sub-title: Buddy's Strawberry Rice Pudding and Summer Tomato Chicken Bake
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a chicken sheet pan dinner with early tomatoes, potatoes and chorizo. Buddy helps pick strawberries for quick jam rice pudding. The summer feast ends with spicy shrimp skewers with homegrown chillies and a refreshing cucumber gazpacho.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Seasons
programme.sub-title: Endless Summer: From Grilled Panzanella Salad to Sour Cherry Tart
programme.desc: Jamie ends summer with crispy duck in a rich plum sauce & roast potatoes. Grilled peppers star in panzanella. Garden zucchini become Mediterranean rice. Sweet finale: sour cherry frangipane with crème fraîche.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Hearty Chicken Pot Pie and French Toast Crumpets with Bacon
programme.desc: From a cosy chicken hotpot with braised cabbage to cheesy French-toast crumpets with smashed peas and bacon, a quick, aromatic fish curry, and a golden pear & almond cake with orange drizzle, Jamie proves simple ingredients really can shine.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Caprese Chicken Burgers and Pork and Fennel Ragu
programme.desc: From crispy, herby Caprese chicken burgers to golden spinach pies, creamy pork & fennel ragù with fresh pappardelle, and a comforting tinned peach cobbler with fruit-rippled cream, Jamie proves budget-friendly cooking can be utterly delicious.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a hearty chicken and veg pie, super-satisfying mushroom risotto, beautiful homemade bread and a no-cook chocolatey fridge cake, all for under a buck a plate.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern goes primal with two antelope dishes: a Greek-style whole leg roasted over the open fire with a lemony avgolemono sauce and crispy potatoes, and a simple antelope tenderloin carpaccio served with roasted tomatoes and Parmesan.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern starts simmering a classic Italian Bolognese sauce, with ground bison, lots of red wine and tomatoes. Then, he adds marinated bison skirt steak tacos with borracho beans to the tailgate menu.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern breaks down a whole rabbit to grill with a spicy, Sichuan sauce and plate with panzanella.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern shares his favorite way to prepare wild turkey breast, pan-fried over an open fire. Served with cheese sauce and jalapeno-bacon wild rice. Then, cooks a simple meal of seared salmon and a side of crispy potatoes and onions.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew butchers a whole pheasant to make yakitori-style skewers and a homemade flatbread, cucumber salad and a mango chutney.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern reimagines the Hawaiian plate lunch with a hand chopped venison burger topped with homemade gravy and a fried egg, jumbo grilled shrimp, a humble macaroni salad and a heaping scoop of white rice.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern champions the often-overlooked mackerel with a main salad of pickled filets, charred tomatoes, grilled frisée, and sweet summer peaches. Then, he grills it whole seasoned with caramelized onions, rutabaga mash and charred grapes.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: The Best Fish You're Not Eating
programme.desc: Andrew takes on "little fish, big fish," cooking grilled whole small fish over Japanese charcoal with sesame spinach, and a larger fish in a bold puttanesca-style dish. Simple techniques, big flavor, and a case for eating lower on the food chain.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Yellowtail Collars & Filets with Miso Sauce
programme.desc: Andrew cooks yellowtail two ways: grilled collars with a sweet, savory Filipino-inspired adobo sauce, and grilled filets with miso sauce, shiso butter, and charred vegetables. A lesson in using the whole fish and getting more from every cut.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Goose, one of Andrew's favorite game birds, is prepared two ways: a French onion soup-style grilled breast sandwich with caramelized onions and melted cheese, and a whole bird, cured, roasted on a beer can, and served with an apple-fennel salad.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: How to Cook a Whole Pig at Home
programme.desc: Andrew tackles suckling pig with two recipes-his take on a three-cup braise with soy, rice wine, and sesame oil, served with plancha fried rice, and a whole pig roasted over open fire and pulled for Philly-style pork sandwiches with broccoli rabe.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Bison Brats & Grilled Kebabs on Flatbread
programme.desc: Andrew butchers a whole bison leg before diving in to grinding meat for bison brats, grilling them up and topping with homemade spicy mustard. Then, he grills Middle Eastern-inspired kebabs to serve over flatbread for a feast built for sharing.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.sub-title: Why LA's Danger Dog is the Best Thing You'll Ever Eat
programme.desc: Kevin & Noah make their twists on some L.A. outdoor classics: Backyard Burgers 2 Ways; Pit-Smoked Pollo Asado; Grilled Mexican Street Corn and Bacon-Wrapped Danger Dogs.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.sub-title: The Only Pulled Pork Recipe You'll Ever Need
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah are smoking up some BBQ Pulled Pork Sandwiches with Coleslaw; frying up a skillet of Creole Cabbage; and charcoal-grilling some Steak Sandwiches with Chimichurri Mayo.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: The Grill Dads bring late-night Mexican food truck eats to your backyard with pollo al pastor tacos, Mission-style carne asada burritos, and mini beef chimichangas.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: Tonight is all about chicken comfort-food dishes: a Gochujang Hot Honey Chicken Sandwich, Almost Buffalo Wings, and a Spicy Chicken Salad Wrap. We're going to light the grills, pour some drinks, and show you how to make 'em in your backyard.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: It's sandwich night! We're making our favorite handheld late-night delights: Chicken Parm Hero, (Not a Philly) Cheesesteak, and Bodega Chopped Cheese. We're going to light the grills, pour some drinks, and show you how to make 'em in your backyard.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.sub-title: These Smoked Turkey Legs are better than the State Fair
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah smoke up some State Fair Turkey Legs; fry some skillet Chorizo & Eggs; smoke-and-fry some Buffalo Rib Tips; and cook up some Sriracha and Garlic-Butter Grilled Shrimp.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.sub-title: The Secret to World-Class Texas Brisket
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah are making brisket 3 ways! They're pit-smoking a classic Texas brisket; showing you how to gussy up a pot of Brisket Baked Beans; and making a charcoal-grilled Vietnamese Brisket Noodle Bowl with all the fixings.

programme.title: Barbecue: Smoke Out
programme.sub-title: Beef Shank is the New Brisket
programme.desc: Kevin and Noah are smoking a humungous Thor's Hammer; cooking up some Catfish & Eggs for breakfast; making a Grilled Salsa Verde; and putting on a pot of Mezcal Charro Beans.

programme.desc: Andrew takes on "little fish, big fish," cooking grilled whole small fish over Japanese charcoal with sesame spinach, and a larger fish in a bold puttanesca-style dish. Simple techniques, big flavor, and a case for eating lower on the food chain.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Yellowtail Collars & Filets with Miso Sauce
programme.desc: Andrew cooks yellowtail two ways: grilled collars with a sweet, savory Filipino-inspired adobo sauce, and grilled filets with miso sauce, shiso butter, and charred vegetables. A lesson in using the whole fish and getting more from every cut.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Goose, one of Andrew's favorite game birds, is prepared two ways: a French onion soup-style grilled breast sandwich with caramelized onions and melted cheese, and a whole bird, cured, roasted on a beer can, and served with an apple-fennel salad.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern starts simmering a classic Italian Bolognese sauce, with ground bison, lots of red wine and tomatoes. Then, he adds marinated bison skirt steak tacos with borracho beans to the tailgate menu.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern breaks down a whole rabbit to grill with a spicy, Sichuan sauce and plate with panzanella.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern shares his favorite way to prepare wild turkey breast, pan-fried over an open fire. Served with cheese sauce and jalapeno-bacon wild rice. Then, cooks a simple meal of seared salmon and a side of crispy potatoes and onions.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew butchers a whole pheasant to make yakitori-style skewers and a homemade flatbread, cucumber salad and a mango chutney.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern reimagines the Hawaiian plate lunch with a hand chopped venison burger topped with homemade gravy and a fried egg, jumbo grilled shrimp, a humble macaroni salad and a heaping scoop of white rice.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern champions the often-overlooked mackerel with a main salad of pickled filets, charred tomatoes, grilled frisée, and sweet summer peaches. Then, he grills it whole seasoned with caramelized onions, rutabaga mash and charred grapes.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: The Best Fish You're Not Eating
programme.desc: Andrew takes on "little fish, big fish," cooking grilled whole small fish over Japanese charcoal with sesame spinach, and a larger fish in a bold puttanesca-style dish. Simple techniques, big flavor, and a case for eating lower on the food chain.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Goose, one of Andrew's favorite game birds, is prepared two ways: a French onion soup-style grilled breast sandwich with caramelized onions and melted cheese, and a whole bird, cured, roasted on a beer can, and served with an apple-fennel salad.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: How to Cook a Whole Pig at Home
programme.desc: Andrew tackles suckling pig with two recipes-his take on a three-cup braise with soy, rice wine, and sesame oil, served with plancha fried rice, and a whole pig roasted over open fire and pulled for Philly-style pork sandwiches with broccoli rabe.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Bison Brats & Grilled Kebabs on Flatbread
programme.desc: Andrew butchers a whole bison leg before diving in to grinding meat for bison brats, grilling them up and topping with homemade spicy mustard. Then, he grills Middle Eastern-inspired kebabs to serve over flatbread for a feast built for sharing.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: Tonight is all about chicken comfort-food dishes: a Gochujang Hot Honey Chicken Sandwich, Almost Buffalo Wings, and a Spicy Chicken Salad Wrap. We're going to light the grills, pour some drinks, and show you how to make 'em in your backyard.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: The Grill Dads bring late-night Mexican food truck eats to your backyard with pollo al pastor tacos, Mission-style carne asada burritos, and mini beef chimichangas.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: It's sandwich night! We're making our favorite handheld late-night delights: Chicken Parm Hero, (Not a Philly) Cheesesteak, and Bodega Chopped Cheese. We're going to light the grills, pour some drinks, and show you how to make 'em in your backyard.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.sub-title: The Secret to Texas-Style Smoked Brisket
programme.desc: Pat and his buddy Leonard Botello use the flavors of the beloved native Post Oak to smoke a perfect Central Texas-style beef brisket.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.desc: Pat Martin travels to a picturesque cattle farm in California's Santa Maria Valley, to hear firsthand from a young pitmaster who has uncovered the secrets of real Santa Maria barbecue.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.sub-title: The Legend of Louis Mueller's Dino Beef Ribs
programme.desc: Pat visits his close friend Wayne Mueller, third-generation pitmaster of Louis Mueller's BBQ, to learn the ins and outs of his famous dino beef ribs.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.sub-title: Pit-Cooked Chicken with Alabama White Sauce
programme.desc: Pat Martin travels to Decatur, Alabama to visit a storied institution of barbecue - Big Bob Gibson's. There, he learns the ins, the outs, and the history of a regional delicacy: pit-cooked chicken with Alabama white sauce.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.desc: Pat Martin teams up with fellow pitmasters to showcase the rugged and nuanced tradition of whole-hog cooking, West Tennessee-style.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.desc: Pat travels to the Emerald Coast of Destin, Florida, to join his friend Adam Evans on a spearfishing adventure, sourcing fresh fish to cook over live fire.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.sub-title: Bluefin Tuna Collars with Chili Pickle Relish
programme.desc: Pat Martin visits chef Adam Perry Lang in Los Angeles, where the pair cooks a prized catch castoff cut - Bluefin Tuna collars.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.sub-title: Catching Gulf Coast Redfish and Blue Crabs
programme.desc: Pat visits Houston to go Gulf Bay fishing with chef Chris Shepherd, hoping to catch some redfish and gather a side of blue crabs.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.desc: Pat visits Edgar Victoria, known for bringing the street foods of Mexico City to Nashville, to do some live fire cooking out in nature as they prepare one of his favorite dishes - skirt steak tlayuda.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.desc: Pat meets the trio behind Blood Bros BBQ to discover how three childhood friends seamlessly blended their Asian-American culture with the Texas barbecue traditions they grew up with.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.sub-title: Hickory Smoked Virginia Ham & Mint Juleps
programme.desc: Pat Martin gets a history lesson on the origin of American barbecue from "The Professor" Tuffy Stone.

programme.title: Barbecue: Life of Fire
programme.desc: Pat Martin links up with his pal, Bill Darsinos, to enjoy a tradition from the heart of Bill's Greek culture when they cook a whole lamb on a spit roast.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Start a new tradition at the dinner table this year with a meat free spin on your favorite main courses. Learn new tips and tricks to make your veggie forward dinner so delightful that even the biggest meat eaters will be back for seconds.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie kicks it up a notch in the Struggle Kitchen with flavor-packed, spicy recipes, and some hot tips for adding layers of flavor. But don't sweat it, these warming dishes won't burn your cash or your taste buds.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: With the help of a friend, Frankie serves up easy and delicious recipes based on take out favorites everyone will enjoy at home. No need to shell out extra cash for delivery fees!

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Winter citrus is here to brighten up your day. Frankie is fixing up some light, zesty, and affordable recipes to add a little seasonal sunshine to your plate.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: From casual weeknights to special occasions, be the star of the dinnertime with these crowd-pleasing meals.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Put leftovers to good use with tasty, waste-reducing recipes that turn excess food into exciting new dishes.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Give your wallet a break by taking the fine-dining experience straight to the comfort of your home. From tender, juicy steak paired with classic sides, to decadent desserts, Frankie's got your date night meal covered.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Treat yourself to a five-star pasta dinner, as Frankie cooks his signature dishes with showstopping sauces.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.sub-title: I Need To Eat Right Now
programme.desc: At the end of a long, hectic day, when you need dinner in a pinch, Frankie unlocks his not-so-secret formula for whipping up quick and easy meals, using minimal ingredients that will satisfy your cravings and feed the family in a flash.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: There may be infinite ways to define pie, but the only math that matters is what makes these sweet and savory dishes fit the bill. In true Struggle Style, Frankie rolls out his best tips and tricks for enjoying hassle-free, cost-friendly pies.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Elk Tenderloin Tostadas and Grilled Rack of Ribs
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern highlights lean elk, first crafting classic Mexican tostadas with grilled tenderloin, avocado, and homemade salsa macha. Then, he grills a whole rack of elk, serving it with creamy colcannon, tarragon emulsion, and rosemary charcoal.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Chicken Fried Deer and Creole Halibut
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares chicken fried axis deer. Next, Chef Zimmern poaches halibut in tomato roux.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Clams Casino and Garlicky Clam Pizza
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern celebrates cherrystone clams with two show-stopping recipes. He prepares classic grilled clams casino with pancetta, then pushes boundaries with an innovative grilled pizza topped with clams, garlic, chiles, and cheese.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Creamy Clam Chowder and Mussels in Spicy Tomato Sauce
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern demystifies cooking clams, mussels and oysters.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Mediterranean Grilled Filets and Salt Crusted Halibut
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern elevates California halibut, first with grilled fillets on saffron labneh with romesco and roasted cabbage. Then, he demonstrates a classic salt-crusted whole halibut, served with whole roasted cauliflower and a rich cream sauce.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern makes two different dishes with red snapper: fried snapper fingers with a spicy chile-orange mayonnaise dipping sauce and a slow-roasted whole fish with a herb salad and charred miso sweet potatoes.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Wild Boar Feast: Chorizo with Mussels and Whole Roasted Leg
programme.desc: Wild boar takes center stage! Andrew makes chorizo with mussels, preserved lemon & white wine. Then, he slow-roasts a whole leg over fire, served Southern-style with pickled cherries, glazed onions & mustard sauce.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Zimmern cooks his version of Peking-style goose. Next, Chef Zimmern cooks a meal of striped bass.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Boar Ribs and Shrimp and Grits
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern makes a homemade barbecue rub, basting sauce and barbecue sauce for wild boar ribs. Next, chef shares his twist on a classic southern shrimp and grits recipe, adding a side of beautiful wild mushrooms.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern showcases quail two ways: classic Southern fried quail with lemon-basil aioli, and as an elevated topping for a traditional open-fire paella, complete with crispy socarrat. He proves this small bird makes an extraordinary dinner.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A 300-year-old Scottish ruin that's been brought back to life. In the Sierra Nevada mountain, an angular home built from zinc and glass. In Sardinia a stunning waterfront villa. And in Germany, a wartime bunker that lay abandoned for 70 years.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Mom and son duo, Paul and Catharine, are overwhelmed by Paul's multi-level backyard. Paul also finds his current indoor fitness regime frustrating. Brian and Sarah muscle up to the challenge by building a gym in his backyard, alongside a hot tub.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Ex-city slickers, Lauren and Mark, miss their friends and family from the city. Brian and Sarah create a country retreat with an outdoor pad, indoor dining lounge, and a chicken coop. When Lauren and Mark's friends visit, they'll never want to leave.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Paris lovers, Louise and Rick, want to share their European enthusiasm with their two daughters without racking up the mileage. Brian and Sarah build them a French-inspired urban escape. It's Paris in their backyard! C'est magnifique!

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Janet's love for her nine grandkids is endless. But so is the chaos they bring inside her home when they come visit. To take that energy outside, Brian and Sarah build a fun kid-zone in the backyard, but also a sophisticated adult area for Janet.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A Norwegian mountain cabin that is so remote it is only accessed by helicopter. A family retreat embedded into rocks in the Californian desert. In Brazil, an epic jungle lodge.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: Explore a palatial cliff-top villa complete with indoor and outdoor pools. A remote cliff-top home deep in West Virginia and then uncover a retreat built into rocks on the rural outskirts of Stockholm.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Steve Langstone and his wife Sharon found their perfect plot in some ancient woodland on the side of a lake, where they wanted to construct a home. They found an innovative company that specializes in constructing homes that are technically moveable.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: The Lambert family have spent the last 9 years searching for a home to call their own. To make building their new home affordable, the family discover a futuristic kit house company in Poland.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: While on holiday in the Outer Hebrides, Ruth Hamilton and John Henry fell in love with the isolated wilderness of the Isle of Harris. To make their new home possible, the couple found an innovative construction company, who specialize in kit houses.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: After years traveling through Europe, Iain and Alyson are ready to put down roots. Their search takes them to Inner Hebridean Island of Skye where they purchase a remote half acre plot, but building a traditional house is a challenge.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Delaval and Veronica are restoring 450 acres for conservation and funding it by building a treehouse in Swanton Novers Wood. A Scottish firm constructs it in 7 sections to minimize damage to the woodland habitat on delivery.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Jamie Feilden's 60-acre farm doubles as a charity for urban youth. To fund it, he now plans two prefabricated elliptical cabins on a remote slope. They'll feature timber decks, open-plan living, and green roofs—though weather may delay the build.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Cool parents, Michelle and Adrian, are passionate about music and events. Brian and Sarah tune into their needs and amp up their boring backyard with a DJ den, a creative arts-and-crafts wall, and hang-out zones for family jam sessions.

programme.desc: Dave and Jorge love art and entertaining, but with tenants in their basement and no creative space inside, Brian and Sarah turn their backyard into a creative party lounge and build a shipping container-inspired structure for an industrial feel.

programme.desc: Medical doctors and busy parents, Cynthia and Boon miss their camping adventures. Brian and Sarah perform some backyard surgery, and build them a cozy campground retreat so they can go straight from the ER to getting some R&R right in their own yard.

programme.title: Backyard Builds

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke arrives in Saigon for a breakfast of iced coffee with condensed milk and a vermicelli noodle dish. Later, he visits his favorite Pho restaurant, then cooks up some spicy Chili Crab Claws.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food journey in Bangkok where he searches for the best Tom Yum soup and entertains the locals with a rare Thai delicacy.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.sub-title: Malaysian Flying Noodles and Pork Bone Soup
programme.desc: In Kuala Lumpur, Luke meets the famous Mr Hung with a reputation for flying instant noodles and Mr Sou who cooks a hearty Pork Bone Soup with medicinal herbal properties.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food adventure exploring Jakarta, a melting pot of diverse cultural cuisines, where he indulges in an Indonesian dessert, the sweet Martabak, known as the "King of Street Food".

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Mamma Mia! Off We Go Again
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred travel to Greece to immerse themselves in the culinary traditions. The taste buddies go island-hopping, keen to learn more about the Mediterranean diet and how the country has one of the healthiest populations in the world.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Go Greek! Clash of the Titans
programme.desc: The boys are in the Greek capital Athens in a vintage VW campervan. This is where high-concept cuisine originated & is still being re-invented, in a city known as the birth place of democracy and the cradle of western civilisation.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: On a mission to taste the worlds best breakfast, the three big egos Gordon, Gino and Fred are venturing out of their comfort zones crossing over the USA boarder into Mexico. They hope to stay out of trouble as they drive their bad boy transporter.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie gets a masterclass in Sicilian sweets and cooking up an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. There's also some chocolaty cannoli.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy

programme.desc: The trio heads to Scotland as Gordon prepares to cook a feast for the chief of Clan Ramsay and his friends. Gordon shows the others how to dress like real Scotsmen as they go in search of Scottish beef, hunt for scallops and play some Highland Games.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Greece, to the island of Skopelos, starting on the mainland in Thessaloniki. Inspired by meze plates and traditional island cuisine he cooks up a smoky aubergine flatbread and a lemony chargrilled chicken with tzatziki and rice.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: We visit the United Kingdom, a nation recognized for its rich history and classic regal elegance, and two hotels that embody the very best of these values despite very different locations.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In Thailand, are the Anantara Golden Triangle and Elephant Camp Resort. Attracting guests who desire luxury and adventure. In the Philippines lies Villa Escudero Plantation and Resort. This picturesque and tranquil Villa is brimming with character.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In this episode we visit two completely different parts of the African continent, both offering guests a unique and contrasting flavour of Africa.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: Today we have two different hotel experiences in the bustling country of Japan. Hotels that combine elements of past and present Japan to create a sophisticated and luxurious experience in the heart of the city.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: We visit two hotels in Eastern Europe which although separated by miles, share the same values of combining their ancient heritage with modern and ground-breaking architecture to deliver their guests a one-of-a-kind experience.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In this episode, we visit Italy - the birthplace of western civilization and home to two incredible hotels set against some of this country's most stunning backdrops which embody the very best of their own regional history and culture.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In Australia, in the popular city of Melbourne are two hotels that attract visitors from around the world. In the heart of the city is the oldest heritage hotel in Australia. And an hour's drive South of the city is the vineyard hotel The Jackalope.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In this episode we're in the nation's capital of India - New Delhi and in 'The City of Lakes' Udaipur at two Oberoi hotels where the standard of service takes hotel hospitality to a whole new level.

programme.desc: We visit two hotels in Eastern Europe which although separated by miles, share the same values of combining their ancient heritage with modern and ground-breaking architecture to deliver their guests a one-of-a-kind experience.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Greece, to the island of Skopelos, starting on the mainland in Thessaloniki. Inspired by meze plates and traditional island cuisine he cooks up a smoky aubergine flatbread and a lemony chargrilled chicken with tzatziki and rice.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie's soaking up inspiration in Spain with tapas, flavor-charged rice dishes, and spectacular seafood. He cooks a stunning pork chop with peppers and aioli and a surf and turf skewer of prawns, mushrooms, and local ham.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Marseille in the south of France. Inspired by their stunning seafood, the traditional flavors of Provence, and the multicultural dishes being served up there, he cooks up a steak with a twist and a sublime courgette and olive tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie explores Tunisia to meet traditional couscous makers, eat delicious street food, and get invited to cook with local chefs on a beach. Inspired by everything he sees, Jamie cooks crispy prawn parcels and a chicken and merguez sausage stew.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: Corsica, the Island of Beauty, is best explored by railway; from Mediterranean waters to towering peaks, stops include Algajola for a seaside dive, Vizzavona on the GR20 trail and historic cities like Corte with its citadel.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: Andalusia, now crossed by high-speed and modernized rail lines, unveils its rich culture and landscapes; along the journey, we visit cities like Seville, Granada with its famed Alhambra and Guadix near the Tabernas Desert.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: The Cinque Terre Express runs along Italy's northern coast east of Genoa; passing through five UNESCO-listed villages, it offers coastal views and access to cobbled streets, harbors, terraced vineyards and pastel-colored houses.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: In Portugal, in the Douro River Valley, a steam locomotive and a small historic train will take you in turn to the water's edge, on the only railroad line in the world that crosses three World Heritage Sites classified by UNESCO.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: In Greece, the Intercity Express travels from Thessaloniki to Athens, passing sites like the Meteora peaks, Delphi ruins and the Acropolis; the journey continues to the Peloponnese on Greece's last cogwheel train.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: The Little Yellow Train winds through the Eastern Pyrenees, crossing the Cerdan plateau, the historic Gisclard railway suspension bridge and the Grandes Canalettes caves, known as the underground Versailles of the Pyrenees.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: In Portugal, in the Douro River Valley, a steam locomotive and a small historic train will take you in turn to the water's edge, on the only railroad line in the world that crosses three World Heritage Sites classified by UNESCO.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Rifle season is off to a slow start, but things are heating up with lots of tags to fill.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: The story of a true old school deer camp meeting new world management practices to take a raw piece of hunting land and develop it into whitetail Mecca.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: With two weeks until the opener of Missouri archery season, Randy and Cody are trying to do everything they can to have the Raven prepped for the first bow camp.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: The story of a true old school deer camp meeting new world management practices to take a raw piece of hunting land and develop it into whitetail Mecca.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Back at the Raven for bow camp, everything is coming together; Randy has a close encounter with one of the OG bucks on the property; Nate closes the deal on a big five-year-old.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Mike heads to Table Rock Lake to meet up with Trenton and his family; even battling the tough fishing conditions following a heavy front, there's never a lack of family time, laughs or friendly competitions with the Southards.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton meets up with Andrew Norbye on Lake Guntersville in Alabama; with not a lot of current being pulled, the deep schools weren't set up to feed, so they go up shallow to fish grass until evening time when the ledge fish really start biting.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Rex meets up with "Mr Crappie" Wally Marshall in Texas, who takes Rex to one of his secret spots to put the hammer on the crappie; not only did they catch crappie, but they also caught some nice largemouth and some top-tier coppernose bluegill.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: The Crew heads to Rivers Lodge and Hunt Club in Kansas to fish strip pits; Trenton and Rex take on Mike and Nate in a three-period mini tournament.

programme.title: The Super Retriever Series Crown Championship
programme.desc: The championship is presented by Eukanuba and features the best retrievers in the world competing in hunting, field trial and hunt-savvy scenarios along with the exuberance of dogs on the dock in the SRS Super Dock competition.

programme.title: The Super Retriever Series Crown Championship
programme.desc: The championship is presented by Eukanuba and features the best retrievers in the world competing in hunting, field trial and hunt-savvy scenarios along with the exuberance of dogs on the dock in the SRS Super Dock competition.

programme.title: HuntStand Presents
programme.desc: Wildfires have consumed thousands of acres across the Bighorn Mountains and the neighboring foothills; maybe the annual HuntStand pronghorn camp could be a total bust, or perhaps the crew will be able to find a few animals beyond the smoke.

programme.title: HuntStand Presents
programme.desc: The West Texas desert has a way of pulling you in.

programme.title: HuntStand Presents
programme.desc: After several years of shifting focus back to his native whitetails, Dahlke returns to mule deer country with the Muley Freak, Erik Van Woerkom.

programme.title: HuntStand Presents
programme.sub-title: Second Chance -- Hunting the November Rut in Ohio
programme.desc: An original film series vividly depicts the sacred experiences, emotions and rewards that come from pursuing wild game in wild places.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.sub-title: Heading North, Missouri Food Plot Prep
programme.desc: On this episode of Hunting Land Man Season 3, Slade and the crew head north to scout out, plan, and put in the plots to lay the groundwork for this falls hunting season in the Midwest!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Slade and the old Trained Assassins crew are headed back to Montrose in pursuit of a big elk. The conditions are not favorable, but the crew is sticking it out!

programme.title: Into the Blue
programme.desc: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger take off from the beautiful Oceans Edge Marina in Key West, Fla.

programme.title: Into the Blue
programme.desc: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger meet up with Brice Barr to head out into the beautiful waters of Gulf in search of giants.

programme.title: Into the Blue
programme.desc: The waters surrounding Ocean's Edge Resort in Key West, Fla., provide no shortage of beautiful fishing areas; Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger meet up with their good friend John Buckheim.

programme.title: Into the Blue
programme.desc: Scott and Steve take off from the Sailfish Marina in Palm Beach, FL, to hunt down big ones in the waters off the Florida coast.

programme.title: Into the Blue
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker and Captain Steve Rodger meet with Dave Fields for a beautifully calm morning at Sailfish Marina; the trio runs into many sport fish species, seeing all the Florida Keys offer.

programme.title: Into the Blue
programme.sub-title: Mahi Smackdown With Captain Josh Krieg
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker reunites with his former mate Captain Josh Krieg for a day offshore of Plantation Boat Mart to chase birds for some mahi-mahi action.

programme.title: The Captain's Log
programme.desc: Captain Jonathan Moss finds himself rooted in history in Apalachicola, Fla., where he's scouting the marsh for hungry backcountry dwellers.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Capt. Jared travels to Venice, Louisiana, where the Mississippi River meets the Gulf, joining Capt. Garrett George and Capt. Jesse LeBoeuf for an action-packed offshore adventure in pursuit of Redfish and Tuna.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: Catching up with an old friend, Carl Parks; Carl's son CJ was killed in a tragic accident in 2018; spending a day of fishing and reflection and celebration of CJ's life.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: TA Mahoney is a classic mom-and-pop store known for going the extra mile and treating everyday people like family; keep an eye on Mike though, you never know what kind of prank is up his sleeve.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: Patriotic Kenny, an 82-year-old Navy veteran with more than 2.7 million TikTok followers, discusses how he gifts mobility scooters to help fellow vets in need.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton goes down North Carolina to learn the family secrets of the Forehand family; Ben Forehand is a guitarist for Christian Contemporary artist Pat Barrett: when Ben isn't playing guitar, he runs Wild and Free Outfitters.

programme.title: Saltwater Underground with Nick Honachefsky
programme.desc: Nick joins Capt. Dave DeGenarro as lightning quick bonito invade New Jersey waters for fast-paced, reel-burning action on light tackle.

programme.title: Local Lines
programme.desc: Capt. Brandon Branch has the opportunity to finally go fish Yankeetown, Fla., like never before with Capt. Trey Mikell, who established himself as one of the best guides in that area.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton makes a trip to Louisiana to fish with Theophile Bourgeois at the Cajun Vista Lodge. With an early rain delay, they took a tour of the lodge and learned about its long-standing family history before hitting the water for some redfish.

programme.title: BaitFuel Fishing Tour
programme.desc: The final qualifier for the season finds the teams in Cardinal, Ontario, taking on the St. Lawrence River. It's the last chance for teams to qualify for the Tour Championship.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Captain Jared Raskob and guest Captain Jimmy Hendricks face challenges from sharks during a fishing trip; the pair adapts their techniques to successfully catch their target species.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the venison that Michelin star chef Mark Robinson has to offer.
